testCases.query: How do I mock LLM API calls in my unit tests?
expectedBehavior: Provides AsyncMock pattern for mocking LLM client responses
Uses unittest.mock.patch to replace the model factory
Emphasizes NEVER calling live LLM APIs in CI
Recommends VCR.py for record/replay in integration tests

testCases.query: Evaluate the quality of my RAG pipeline output
expectedBehavior: Uses DeepEval with multiple quality metrics (relevancy, faithfulness, hallucination)
Sets appropriate thresholds: relevancy >= 0.7, faithfulness >= 0.8, hallucination <= 0.3
Tests with LLMTestCase including input, actual_output, and retrieval_context
Validates structured output with Pydantic schema before quality scoring

testCases.query: Set up VCR.py for deterministic LLM integration tests in CI
expectedBehavior: Configures VCR with record_mode='none' in CI, 'new_episodes' locally
Filters sensitive headers (authorization, x-api-key) from cassettes
Uses pytest.mark.vcr decorator for clean test integration
Ensures deterministic replay without network access in CI

testCases.query: Test that my LLM always returns valid structured JSON output
expectedBehavior: Validates output with Pydantic BaseModel schema
Tests field constraints (min_length, ge/le bounds) not just presence
Covers edge cases: empty input, long input, unicode, schema violations
Asserts more than 'is not None' — uses full schema validation

testCases.query: Write Playwright E2E tests for my checkout page
expectedBehavior: Should NOT apply LLM testing patterns to a browser E2E test
Should redirect to testing-e2e skill for Playwright-based testing
Does not suggest DeepEval, VCR.py, or LLM mocking for UI tests
